subject: fix(proposals): counterparty-accept now replaces module content (parity with creator-accept)
body:
Bug surfaced by the `copy` trigger audit. Left unfixed in the copy
commit because the symptom was a toast inconsistency but the root
cause was a missing state mutation — a behaviour bug, not a copy
issue.

The proposal flow:

1. Counterparty creates Proposal A (proposed_by: counterparty.id,
   proposed_content: their change request).
2. Creator reviews. Three resolutions available:
   - Accept → updateModule({ content: A.proposed_content }) +
     resolveProposal(A, 'accepted'). Module becomes A's body. ✓
   - Reject → resolveProposal(A, 'rejected'). No content change.
   - Counter → resolveProposal(A, 'countered', <creator's text>) AND
     createProposal(…) as Proposal B (proposed_by: creator.id,
     proposed_content: creator's counter text).
3. Counterparty reviews Proposal B. Can accept / reject / counter.

The creator's accept-path (ModuleEditorPage:169-172) correctly does
updateModule + resolveProposal before the toast. The counterparty's
accept-path (lines 312-315 pre-fix) ONLY resolved the proposal —
never called updateModule. Accepting the creator's counter was a
no-op on module content.

ProposalCard gates the Accept button with `status === 'pending' &&
!isOwnProposal`, so this path only fires when the counterparty is
accepting a proposal they didn't author. In practice that means
they're accepting a creator-authored counter, and
`proposal.proposed_content` is the target body — same semantics as
the creator branch accepting a counterparty-authored original.

Fix: mirror the creator branch — updateModule +
resolveProposal + matching toast. Added a brief in-code comment
explaining the symmetry so a future reviewer sees why both branches
write updateModule before resolveProposal.

Removed the corresponding `## Behaviour Asymmetry` entry from
docs/TODO.md since it's fixed.

No new test: ModuleEditorPage doesn't have a component test yet (both
creator and counterparty branches share ProposalCard + useProposals +
useModules, which would all need mocking for an end-to-end accept
assertion). The fix is one line mirroring an existing working
pattern; verified against the creator branch's contract in the
audit above.

Tests 155/155 passing, tsc -b clean, eslint clean.

Tags: bug-fix, proposals, domain-logic
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: frontend-audit-sweep
Semver: patch
